data.text: The first search showed I was in the execution wrapper, not the Go repo root, so I’m switching into `cli/` and reading the exact GraphQL implementations plus any existing tests around these symbols. That will tell me which symbols are genuinely dead versus just missing a production entrypoint.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the package has real implementations, so now I’m checking how the server constructs the resolver and whether the flagged helpers are production-only or test-only. The main question is whether the deadcode result is a missing edge in the server wiring or an actually obsolete helper.

data.text: I found the server does instantiate `graphql.Resolver`, so I’m checking whether the deadcode report is about constructors/helpers that are only reachable through gqlgen-generated code. If that’s the case, the fix is wiring the actual server path, not changing the GraphQL methods themselves.

data.text: I found the gqlgen server construction path, so the remaining work is to classify the five flagged symbols. One of them may be a stale exported constructor, and the others are likely test-only helpers or cache hooks that the runtime no longer needs.
